_meta.description: Soft memory stores user preferences that influence writing style but are not hard constraints. These are learned patterns that can be overridden by explicit user instructions.
_meta.usage: The AI reads this file during the RECALL step. Preferences here guide style choices but do not override explicit user instructions in the current session.
_meta.maintenance: The AI updates this file when it observes consistent user preferences across sessions. Changes are additive; old preferences are kept but can be marked as deprecated.

target_word_count.note: Dense paragraphs, one core idea each. Extracted from own paper set.

paragraph_preferences.opening_sentence_note: Each paragraph opens with a sentence stating its purpose or context. Common patterns: 'To [verb]..., this study [verb]s...' or 'The [COMPONENT] module is designed to [PURPOSE].'
paragraph_preferences.closing_sentence_note: No single dominant pattern. Sometimes transition, sometimes summary, sometimes equation reference.
paragraph_preferences.internal_flow_note: 1. Purpose/context (1-2 sentences, often a 'To [verb]...' clause) -> 2. Method statement or approach -> 3. Technical detail, formulation, or step-by-step procedure. NOT a rigid motivation->formulation->definition template.

first_person_en.count_per_section: 0 — all 5 own papers use zero first-person pronouns
first_person_en.self_reference_note: 'this study' is dominant (4/5 papers); Paper 01 prefers 'this paper'. Never 'we/our/us'.

high_frequency.note: Dominant sentence-opening device across all 5 own papers (54+ total instances). E.g., 'To capture...', 'To address...', 'To represent...'.
Primary back-reference connector (34+ total). Format depends on journal: 'Fig. X' (3/5 papers) or 'Figure X' (2/5 papers).

medium_frequency.note: 12 instances, concentrated in Paper 04. Signature participial bridge.
Used for contrast with prior work.

equation_preferences.post_where_note: Interpretation typically comes in the next paragraph, not immediately after the where block.

cross_reference_preferences.equation_format_note: Papers 01-04 use 'Equation (N)'; Paper 05 uses 'Eq. (N)'. Follow target journal style.
cross_reference_preferences.figure_format_note: Papers 03-05 use 'Fig. X'; Papers 01-02 use 'Figure X'. Determined by journal convention, not a fixed preference.

typical_order: Physics/Mechanical Modeling (if applicable — present in 3/5 own papers)
section_ordering_preference.note: The true invariant is: framework overview first, then module-by-module in pipeline order. Physics-before-architecture holds when a physics module exists (Papers 01-03) but is NOT universal — Papers 04 and 05 have no physics/mechanics section.

humanizer_preference.note: Always ask before applying humanizer. User has not expressed a default preference yet.

dominant_verbs._note: Most frequent technical verbs across all 5 own papers, ranked by total count.

technical_verb_preferences.avoid_note: Previously 'utilize' and 'leverage' were on the avoid list, but both appear in own papers (utilize: Papers 04/05; leverage: Paper 04). Moved to acceptable usage.
